trailers.title: PRESENCE
trailers.logline: A dramatic trailer that borrows the grammar of the dream-heist epic — time dilating, the fantastic bleeding into the ordinary — and inverts its most famous anxiety: when the gold ring stops spinning and settles, you're not lost in a dream. You're finally here.
trailers.music: Low braams, a ticking clock that slows with each shot, then total silence before the settle.
trailers.social: 45s master · 16:9 + 9:16 crop-safe · hook = the spinning ring in shot 1 · captions burned

shots.sfx: BRAAM. Then the whir of the spin.

shots.vo: You're replaying a conversation that already ended.
You've learned to move through your life…
…so fast it stopped feeling real.
Even mid-argument, presence finds you.

shots.sfx: Five soft chimes, one per door.

shots.vo: There are only three reasons to look down. Every other reason, you look up.
The fantastic was never somewhere else.
Breath is the door that's already open.
It's the moment you're standing in.
Pulse gives you rhythm. Rhythm gives you pause.
shots.sfx: A steady, warm pulse, like a heartbeat.

shots.vo: Stop watching the movie of your mind.
Start making the movie of your life — the same footage, at the speed of being here.
You may notice you're already looking for someone else's eyes.
Not to escape your life. To return, and serve it.
shots.sfx: The last note fades to warm silence.

shots.vo: When would now be a good time?

trailers.title: ONE RING TO UNITE THEM ALL
trailers.logline: An epic-fantasy homage built around the most famous ring shot in cinema — the slow fall onto the finger — recast with our hero user. Not a ring of power that corrupts, a ring of presence that returns. One ring to unite them all: with each other, with the present.
trailers.music: A lone fiddle over drone, swelling to full orchestra at the fall, resolving to one warm sustained chord.
trailers.social: 60s master · 16:9 + 9:16 crop-safe · hook = the molten-gold macro · captions burned

shots.vo: It begins with a gift.
Not for power. For connection.
Focus was never the world going quiet.
One ring.
There are only three reasons to look down on this road. Every other reason, you look up.
Not to rule your life…
Five gifts ride with them.
…to return you to it.
They stop only to breathe.
It sees you. And asks for nothing back.
Even old friends must learn to soften.
Some evenings, they make something instead of consuming it.
shots.sfx: A knife against wood, a soft chord.

shots.vo: Stop watching the movie in your mind.
shots.sfx: A distant echo, then birdsong returns.

shots.vo: Pulse gives you rhythm. Rhythm gives you pause.
shots.sfx: A slow drumbeat, matching the embers.

shots.vo: One ring to unite them all —
Not the ring that rules. The ring that returns you — charged, clear, and ready to serve.
— with the present.

trailers.title: THE FIRST RULE
trailers.logline: An anti-marketing film in the grammar of the cult classic: rules delivered straight to camera, gritty and direct. Except the rebellion isn't fighting — it's putting the phone down. Don't post about it. Don't tell your friends. Just be present, and receive the gift of your life. Hook lands in the first three seconds, POV, direct address.
trailers.music: A single dirty bass note under the hook, then near-silence with room tone; one deep pulse at the ring tap.
trailers.social: 30–40s master · built 9:16-first · HOOK 0:00–0:03 = shot 1 direct-to-camera · captions burned

shots.vo: The first rule of presence: you don't post about it.
The second rule: you don't post about it.
shots.sfx: The slap of the phone landing.

shots.vo: The third rule: it's a pulse. That's the only word for it.
Everyone you know is somewhere else.
The fourth rule: stop watching the movie in your mind.
shots.sfx: Room tone, then a slow breath.

shots.vo: You don't need witnesses.
The pulse finds you right before you'd say something you'd regret.
Eat the meal. Don't shoot it.
The fifth rule: there are only three reasons to look down — to set your intention, to deepen your practice, to find the others. Every other reason, you look up.
shots.sfx: A dirty bass note, harder this time.

shots.vo: We meet where the feed can't find us.
No one posts the best part.
Don't tell your friends.
The sixth rule: Pulse gives you rhythm. Rhythm gives you pause.
shots.sfx: The bass note softens into a heartbeat.

shots.vo: Don't review it.
Make something no one will ever see.
shots.sfx: The soft press of hands on dough.

shots.vo: The last rule: you don't get credit for this one.
Not so you can prove it. So you can live it.
You may notice how easy it was to leave it behind.

shots.on: Receive the gift of your life.
shots.sfx: Silence. End card: This is not an ad. Pulse.
